methods.description: Gene-level expression analysis was performed to identify differentially expressed genes among experimental groups using the limma package in R 3.5.1. Genes with a log 2 fold change higher than 1 or lower than −1 and a p-value less than 0.05 were considered differentially expressed.

steps.description: Gene-level expression analysis was carried out with R 3.5.1 software using limma package.
steps.searchable_summary: perform gene-level expression analysis using limma package in R

steps.description: Those genes with a log 2 fold change higher than 1 or lower than −1 and with a p-value less than 0.05 were considered as differentially expressed among the experimental groups.
steps.searchable_summary: filter genes based on log 2 fold change and p-value

methods.description: Significant signals from metabolomics data between Active and Placebo groups were visualized using a heat map with hierarchical clustering to depict differences in metabolite levels.

steps.description: Significant signals from metabolomics between Active (n = 8) and Placebo (n = 14) groups at T2 were depicted on a heat map using hierarchical clustering of the samples (represented in columns) and metabolites (in rows).
steps.searchable_summary: create heat map with hierarchical clustering for metabolomics data

steps.description: Red cells represent higher levels of the specific metabolite in that sample, whereas blue cells represent lower levels. Samples and metabolites are clustered according to their similarity.
steps.searchable_summary: color code heat map cells based on metabolite levels

methods.description: Mann-Whitney U test with a Benjamini-Hochberg correction was used to detect statistical significance in the metabolomics data.

steps.description: Mann-Whitney U test with a Benjamini-Hochberg correction was used to detect statistical significance (P < .05).
steps.searchable_summary: perform Mann-Whitney U test with Benjamini-Hochberg correction
